spec_match.rationale: Walks through edge-CDN injecting Speculation-Rules header on behalf of customer with restrictive CSP — the motivating use case.

interactive.rationale: Mostly a walkthrough; visitor steps through stages but doesn't see live Chrome behaviour. No real fetch with the header is issued.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises the edge-CDN flow walkthrough; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't surface the alternative paths (CDN could ALSO add a script-src to CSP or use a nonce). Walkthrough framing implies one true path.

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/5113249462550528 — WRONG feature ID. This belongs to feature 5123809745829888. Critical rule #3 regression.

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: See also points at chromestatus.com/feature/5113249462550528 but this concept belongs to feature 5123809745829888. Major rule #3 regression.

openQuestions.title: Show alternative CDN strategies
openQuestions.detail: Add a comparison panel: 'inject Speculation-Rules header' vs 'inject script-src CSP' vs 'rewrite to use nonce'. Visitors get the full picture.

summary: Edge-CDN walkthrough hits the motivating use case but ships the WRONG chromestatus feature ID and is mostly narrative — no real fetch demonstrates the verdict change.
